nws_forecast.name: Today
nws_forecast.detailed: Rain and patchy fog before 8am, then rain and patchy fog and a slight chance of thunderstorms between 8am and 11am, then rain likely and a chance of thunderstorms and patchy fog between 11am and 1pm, then a chance of thunderstorms and a chance of rain between 1pm and 2pm, then rain likely and a chance of thunderstorms. Cloudy, with a high near 60. South wind around 16 mph, with gusts as high as 33 mph. Chance of precipitation is 100%. New rainfall amounts between three quarters and one inch possible.

nws_forecast.name: Tonight
nws_forecast.detailed: Rain before 8pm, then rain and patchy fog. Cloudy. Low around 39, with temperatures rising to around 52 overnight. South wind 16 to 23 mph, with gusts as high as 61 mph. Chance of precipitation is 100%. New rainfall amounts between a half and three quarters of an inch possible.

nws_forecast.name: Tuesday
nws_forecast.short_forecast: Slight Chance Light Rain then Mostly Sunny
nws_forecast.detailed: A slight chance of rain before 7am. Mostly sunny, with a high near 44. West wind 14 to 17 mph, with gusts as high as 33 mph. Chance of precipitation is 20%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.

nws_forecast.name: Tuesday Night
nws_forecast.detailed: Mostly clear, with a low around 23. West wind around 14 mph, with gusts as high as 28 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Wednesday
nws_forecast.detailed: Mostly sunny, with a high near 38. West wind 6 to 12 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Wednesday Night
nws_forecast.detailed: Mostly cloudy, with a low around 29. South wind around 7 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Thursday
nws_forecast.detailed: Mostly sunny, with a high near 43. South wind 6 to 10 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Thursday Night
nws_forecast.short_forecast: Partly Cloudy then Slight Chance Light Snow
nws_forecast.detailed: A slight chance of snow between 3am and 5am, then a slight chance of rain. Partly cloudy, with a low around 33. South wind around 9 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Friday
nws_forecast.detailed: A slight chance of rain before 4pm. Partly sunny, with a high near 50. Southwest wind around 10 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Friday Night
nws_forecast.short_forecast: Mostly Cloudy then Slight Chance Rain And Snow
nws_forecast.detailed: A slight chance of rain between 1am and 2am, then a slight chance of rain and snow. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 32. West wind around 10 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Saturday
nws_forecast.detailed: A chance of rain and snow. Partly sunny, with a high near 46. Northwest wind around 12 mph. Chance of precipitation is 40%. New snow accumulation of less than half an inch possible.

nws_forecast.name: Saturday Night
nws_forecast.detailed: A chance of rain and snow. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 30. West wind around 12 mph. Chance of precipitation is 40%. New snow accumulation of less than one inch possible.

nws_forecast.name: Sunday
nws_forecast.detailed: A chance of snow before 5pm, then a chance of rain and snow. Mostly cloudy, with a high near 43. Northwest wind around 13 mph. Chance of precipitation is 30%. New snow accumulation of around one inch possible.

nws_forecast.name: Sunday Night
nws_forecast.detailed: A chance of rain and snow before 4am. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 29. West wind around 13 mph. New snow accumulation of less than half an inch possible.

nws_alerts.areaDesc: Hartford; Tolland; Windham; Western Franklin; Eastern Franklin; Northern Worcester; Central Middlesex County; Western Essex; Eastern Essex; Western Hampshire; Western Hampden; Eastern Hampshire; Eastern Hampden; Southern Worcester; Western Norfolk; Southeast Middlesex; Suffolk; Eastern Norfolk; Northern Bristol; Western Plymouth; Eastern Plymouth; Southern Bristol; Southern Plymouth; Barnstable; Northwest Middlesex County; Northwest Providence; Southeast Providence; Western Kent; Eastern Kent; Bristol; Washington; Newport
nws_alerts.headline: Flood Watch issued March 16 at 1:50AM EDT until March 17 at 8:00AM EDT by NWS Boston/Norton MA
nws_alerts.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by heavy rainfall continues to be possible.

* WHERE...Portions of northern Connecticut, including the following
areas, Hartford CT, Tolland CT and Windham CT, Massachusetts,
including the following areas, Barnstable MA, Central Middlesex
MA, Eastern Essex MA, Eastern Franklin MA, Eastern Hampden MA,
Eastern Hampshire MA, Eastern Norfolk MA, Eastern Plymouth MA,
Northern Bristol MA, Northern Middlesex MA, Northern Worcester MA,
Southeast Middlesex MA, Southern Bristol MA, Southern Plymouth MA,
Southern Worcester MA, Suffolk MA, Western Essex MA, Western
Franklin MA, Western Hampden MA, Western Hampshire MA, Western
Norfolk MA and Western Plymouth MA, and Rhode Island, including
the following areas, Bristol RI, Eastern Kent RI, Newport RI,
Northwest Providence RI, Southeast Providence RI, Washington RI
and Western Kent RI.

* WHEN...From 5 AM EDT early this morning through Tuesday morning.

* IMPACTS...Excessive runoff from heavy rainfall may result in
flooding of rivers, creeks, streams, and other low-lying and
flood-prone locations. Flooding may occur in poor drainage and
urban areas. Area creeks and streams are running high and could
flood with more heavy rain.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
nws_alerts.instruction:
You should monitor later forecasts and be alert for possible Flood
Warnings. Those living in areas prone to flooding should be prepared
to take action should flooding develop.

NWSheadline: FLOOD WATCH REMAINS IN EFFECT FROM 5 AM EDT EARLY THIS MORNING THROUGH TUESDAY MORNING

nws_alerts.areaDesc: Central Middlesex County; Western Essex; Eastern Essex; Western Norfolk; Southeast Middlesex; Suffolk; Eastern Norfolk; Northern Bristol; Western Plymouth; Eastern Plymouth; Southern Bristol; Southern Plymouth; Barnstable; Dukes; Nantucket; Northwest Providence; Southeast Providence; Western Kent; Eastern Kent; Bristol; Washington; Newport; Block Island
nws_alerts.headline: High Wind Warning issued March 16 at 12:11AM EDT until March 17 at 6:00AM EDT by NWS Boston/Norton MA
nws_alerts.description:
* WHAT...South winds 25 to 35 mph with gusts up to 65 mph expected.

* WHERE...Portions of eastern, northeastern, and southeastern
Massachusetts and northern and southern Rhode Island.

* WHEN...From 5 PM this afternoon to 6 AM EDT Tuesday.

* IMPACTS...Damaging winds will blow down trees and power lines.
Power outages are possible. Travel will be difficult, especially
for high profile vehicles.
nws_alerts.instruction:
Remain in the lower levels of your home during the windstorm, and
avoid windows. Watch for falling debris and tree limbs. Use caution
if you must drive.

NWSheadline: HIGH WIND WARNING IN EFFECT FROM 5 PM THIS AFTERNOON TO 6 AM EDT TUESDAY
